assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an email address.
The developer has not yet created a Stripe account, API keys, or Checkout integration.
Sandbox or test-mode work is acceptable for first success; live-mode KYC activation is out of scope unless selected.

developer_goal: Create a Stripe account and complete the earliest official first-success path to a working test checkout or authenticated API result.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved among hosted Checkout test payment, first Customer API create, and CLI anonymous sandbox provisioning.
first_success.observable_signal: Official docs name Congratulations for Checkout, a created Customer for the API-request guide, and working sandbox keys for the CLI route, without one corpus-wide winner.

route_selection.surface: Stripe Dashboard account creation plus official Get started / Checkout quickstart docs
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete account-to-first-success route only after the terminal outcome and implementation surface are fixed.

candidates.name: Hosted Checkout quickstart after Stripe account creation
candidates.reason: Get started links Checkout for developers ready to build, but language and framework tabs change install, env, and run steps without naming one canonical tab.

candidates.name: Send your first Stripe API request (create Customer)
candidates.reason: Reaches an authenticated API success with fewer integration steps than Checkout, but is a different first-success type than a payment.

candidates.name: Stripe CLI anonymous sandbox without account registration
candidates.reason: Official Get started text offers sandbox keys with no account registration, which conflicts with an account-creation starting boundary.

required_path.action: Create a Stripe account from the official registration entry linked by Get started.

required_fields.label: Account registration fields
required_fields.notes: Public docs confirm account creation is required for Dashboard keys, but the register page did not expose a field-level form in the fetch (browser-compatibility gate).

required_path.observable_result: The developer can open a Stripe Dashboard or sandbox context with test API keys.

required_path.action: Retrieve test or sandbox publishable and secret API keys for the selected environment.
required_path.observable_result: Test API keys are available for server and client configuration.

required_path.action: Complete the selected official first-success implementation branch (Checkout language tab, API Customer create, or CLI sandbox).
required_path.observable_result: The selected official terminal signal is reached.

prerequisites.description: A local runtime matching the chosen Checkout language or framework tab is required if the Checkout quickstart is selected.

external_gates.description: Live-mode payments require business verification and KYC checklist completion; sandbox testing does not.

platform_outcomes.description: Stripe generates test and live API key pairs after account creation.
Sandbox environments allow simulated transactions without moving money.

excluded.item: Live account activation and business verification checklist
excluded.reason: Official docs place KYC after sandbox testing and require it only to leave sandbox for live services.

excluded.item: Reading all product quickstarts and use-case guides
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is excluded from required developer actions.

sources.title: Send your first Stripe API request
evidence_supported: API key pairs after account creation

sources.title: Stripe Dashboard register
evidence_supported: Public fetch hit browser-compatibility gate without field inventory

sources.title: Set up your account
evidence_supported: Live KYC and business verification requirements

uncertainties.question: Which first-success outcome is the corpus route: hosted Checkout test payment, first Customer API create, or CLI sandbox without registration?
uncertainties.impact: The routes differ in required actions, fields, implementation surface, and whether account creation is even required.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A human selection of one bounded developer goal and terminal signal, then a fresh-account walkthrough of that single route.

uncertainties.question: If Checkout is selected, which language or framework tab is canonical?
uncertainties.impact: Install commands, project files, env vars, and run steps change by tab.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official designation of one default tab, or an explicit corpus policy choosing one.

uncertainties.question: What exact Stripe signup fields and verification challenges appear for a fresh account today?
uncertainties.impact: Account-step field counts remain unverified behind the Dashboard registration UI.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated screenshots or a read-only walkthrough of dashboard.stripe.com/register from a fresh session.
